name: Rainbow Serpent
description: The Rainbow Serpent is a foundational creature in Aboriginal Australian mythology, representing creation, life, water, fertility, and cyclical regeneration. It is a powerful and often ambiguous figure, capable of both creation and destruction. Depictions vary across different Aboriginal groups, but it is generally conceived as a massive snake or serpent of immense size and vibrant colours that embodies the spirit of the land and its waterways. The Rainbow Serpent is believed to have shaped the landscape during the Dreamtime, carving out rivers, gorges, and waterholes, and is often associated with rain, storms, and the origin of life. It acts as a law-giver, punishing those who break tribal customs and rewarding those who uphold them. The Serpent's presence is felt in the natural world, particularly in bodies of water and during significant weather events. Respect for the Rainbow Serpent is paramount, as it is seen as a guardian of the land and a source of spiritual power. Disrespect can bring about drought, floods, or other forms of misfortune. It is a symbol of continuity, connection to the land, and the enduring power of Aboriginal culture.
subtitle: Creator, destroyer, and life-giver.
appearance: A massive serpent of iridescent colours, often described as shimmering with all the colours of the rainbow. Its size is immense, capable of spanning vast distances. Some depictions include features like a kangaroo's head or a fish's tail, reflecting the diversity of its representation across different Aboriginal cultures.
habitat: Waterholes, rivers, billabongs, and other bodies of water. It is also associated with the sky and the underground, representing the connection between different realms.
behavior: The Rainbow Serpent is a powerful and often unpredictable force. It can be benevolent, providing water and fertility, but it can also be destructive, bringing floods and storms. It acts as a guardian of the land and its laws, punishing those who violate them. It is often associated with initiation ceremonies and the transmission of knowledge.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Landscape
keyMyths.description: The Rainbow Serpent emerged from the earth during the Dreamtime and traveled across the land, carving out rivers, gorges, and waterholes with its immense body, shaping the landscape as we know it. This journey established the sacred sites and waterways that are central to Aboriginal culture.

sources.author: Ronald M. Berndt
sources.description: An overview of Aboriginal religious beliefs and practices, including the significance of the Rainbow Serpent.
sources.text: An overview of Aboriginal religious beliefs and practices, including the significance of the Rainbow Serpent.

sources.author: Catherine H. Berndt
